channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements to buy them in kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hunter Biden hires Alex Murdaugh’s lawyer in latest court case; Abbe Lowell out
item.description: South Carolina attorney Richard A. Harpootlian is Hunter Biden's new counsel, as Abbe Lowell has reportedly been dropped from a defamation case being heard in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE says it needs to know the government’s most sensitive data, but can’t say why
item.description: DOGE staffers have skirted privacy laws, training and security protocols to gain virtually unfettered access to financial and personal information stored in siloed government databases.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler and Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live: Intelligence officials face House following heated Senate hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks shutdown of US-funded radio network
item.description: A Washington, D.C., federal judge granted Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty a temporary restraining order in lawsuit against Donald Trump adviser Kari Lake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: In their own words: Trump officials shrugging off Signal leak once decried Clinton's server
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The attempts by President Donald Trump and top leaders of his administration to downplay a security breach that revealed military strike plans in a Signal group chat including a journalist stand in stark contrast to their reaction to Hillary Clinton's use of a home server as secret
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Energy and migration will top Rubio's agenda on his Caribbean visit this week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Weaning Caribbean countries from their dependence on Venezuelan oil and combating illegal immigration will top Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s tour of three Caribbean nations this week, a visit that comes as the Trump administration increasingly focuses its attention on the Wester
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Council to vote on new rules for scaffolding, sidewalk sheds
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council is set to take up a series of bills aimed at cracking down on scaffolding and sidewalk sheds.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Debt limit time crunch could test both parties
item.description: House Democrats are sending an early warning shot as the government’s borrowing window inches to a close: winning Democratic support for a debt-ceiling hike will come at a cost for Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Greg Abbott showing no rush to replace late U.S. Rep. Sylvester Turner
item.description: Republicans hold a tiny majority in the House, creating an incentive for Abbott to hold off on calling an election for Turner’s seat, which would likely be filled by a Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Obama vs Trump: Political giants back opposing candidates in widely-watched Wisconsin Supreme Court contest
item.description: Former President Barack Obama and President Donald Trump have lined up on opposite sides of Wisconsin's state Supreme Court contest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court takes up $8 billion phone and internet subsidy for rural and low-income areas
item.description: The Supreme Court is hearing arguments in a major legal fight over the $8 billion a year the federal government spends to subsidize phone and internet services in schools, libraries and rural areas, in a new test of federal regulatory power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US intelligence officials to appear at House hearing after Senate grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's top intelligence officials will brief House lawmakers Wednesday on global threats facing the U.S. — though they'll likely be questioned again over their use of a group text to discuss plans for military strikes in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump intel officials testify on threat from drug cartels as Dems press them on leak of attack plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's top intelligence officials stressed to Congress the threat they said was posed by international criminal gangs, drug cartels and human smuggling, testifying in a hearing Tuesday that unfolded against the backdrop of a security breach involving the mistake
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge contends Nazis got more due process than Trump deportees did
item.description: The fight over the rarely used wartime power has become central to Trump’s immigration crackdown agenda and his efforts to stretch the powers of the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Eichorn tried to obstruct investigation and lied about having a gun, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors are asking a judge to keep former Minnesota senator Justin Eichorn in jail, alleging he tried to keep them from accessing a computer and cell phone in his St. Paul apartment. They also alleged he lied about having a gun in the apartment.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Nassau Democrats block County Executive Blakeman's $400M plan
item.description: LONG ISLAND, N.Y. (PIX11) – Nassau Democrats have accused County Executive Bruce Blakeman of withholding critical first responder funding for their districts – and have refused to approve his $400 million plan for the county.
item.dc:creator: Kirstin Cole, PIX 11

item.title: Florida Democrats continue to push for Medicaid expansion
item.description: Amid fears of federal cuts, the battle continues at the statehouse as Florida Democrats continue to push the GOP super majority to consider expanding Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After ‘incredibly unusual’ start, Minnesota lawmakers just want to get back to normal
item.description: The 70 days since the legislative session started have seen a variety of twists and turns that have left three seats open at various points in time. Now, leaders hope for a boring phase ahead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Letter carriers speak out against Trump plans to privatize U.S. Postal Service
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul and Duluth on Sunday against calls to privatize the U.S. Postal Service. U.S. Sen. Tina Smith called the move a “hostile takeover” by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: 2 months into Trump's second administration, the news industry faces challenges from all directions
item.description: During the first Trump administration, many journalists worried most about being called “fake news.” They now face a more assertive President Donald Trump in his second term as a blitz of action by his new administration has journalists on their heels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DOJ creating path for people with criminal convictions to own guns again
item.description: The Justice Department plans to create a process for those with criminal convictions to restore their gun rights, sparking alarm it will return firearms to those convicted of violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
